steps.title: Set up and simplify the budget inequality
steps.content:
With $a$ attendees, the total cost is $40 + 11.50a$. To stay within the budget of $225$:
$$40 + 11.50a \leq 225$$
Subtract the venue fee from both sides:
$$11.50a \leq 185$$
Divide both sides by 11.50:
$$a \leq \frac{185}{11.50}$$

steps.title: Calculate the maximum whole number of attendees
steps.content:
Evaluating the division: $\frac{185}{11.50} \approx 16.087$

Since the number of attendees must be a whole number and we need the greatest number without exceeding the budget, the answer is $a = 16$. Verification: $40 + 11.50(16) = 224 \leq 225$, which is within the budget.
